description:
Advances: specled.branch_guard, specled.implementation_tier

Deliverable:
Full detail: refined plan Stage 2 (mini-plan think-q2h run 20260701-214533) + decision specled.decision.realization_tiers_nil_default. Depends on S1 (specled_-s65.1).
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex: run/3 loads Config.load(root) once and passes the struct down; refactor severity_opts/2 to take the already-loaded config (removes the double Config.load this change would otherwise introduce). run_realization/3: when config.realization.enabled_tiers is a list, pass enabled_tiers: to Orchestrator.run_with_attestations/2; when nil, omit the key entirely so the orchestrator default tier set applies (backward-compat invariant). Emit one branch_guard_realization_unknown_tier finding per entry in config.realization.rejected via the existing emit/6 + Severity.resolve path; add the code to the per-code defaults at warning. Message: realization.enabled_tiers contains unknown tier <token>; valid tiers: api_boundary, implementation, expanded_behavior, typespecs, use — this tier will not run.
- Implementation caution: verify explicit enabled_tiers: [] cannot clobber committed hashes via refresh_and_commit_hashes/3 with empty binding lists; if HashStore.write/2 would replace rather than merge, guard the commit when the enabled set is empty and cover with a test.
- lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex: moduledoc only — update the implementation-tier opt-in section to name realization.enabled_tiers in .spec/config.yml as the consumer-facing path (threaded by SpecLedEx.BranchCheck); retain the AST-shape caveat. No code changes in this module.
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md: new must requirements specled.branch_guard.realization_tiers_from_config (spec.check realization run uses realization.enabled_tiers when present, orchestrator default when absent) and specled.branch_guard.realization_unknown_tier_finding (rejected names emit branch_guard_realization_unknown_tier at default warning, respecting severity overrides and off) + scenarios + tagged_tests verification coverage.
- .spec/specs/implementation_tier.spec.md: new must requirement specled.implementation_tier.config_opt_in (enabled_tiers including implementation causes the implementation tier to run during spec.check branch check) + scenario + tagged_tests coverage.
- Regenerate .spec/state.json.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: new test/specled_ex/branch_check/realization_config_test.exs, modeled on test/specled_ex/realization/implication_integration_test.exs fixtures. Cases: (1) enabled test — tmp git-repo fixture with impl-friendly bindings and config realization.enabled_tiers: [api_boundary, implementation]; assert the implementation tier ran (implementation drift finding on a mutated closure or implementation entries in hash-store/attestations); tag specled.implementation_tier.config_opt_in and specled.branch_guard.realization_tiers_from_config. (2) absent-section test — same fixture without realization section; assert pre-change tier set ran, no implementation-tier activity; tag specled.branch_guard.realization_tiers_from_config. (3) rejected-tier test — bogus tier name; assert branch_guard_realization_unknown_tier finding at warning and that a severities override to off absorbs it; tag specled.branch_guard.realization_unknown_tier_finding. Small shared-fixture edits to test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs allowed.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: the scenarios authored in this stage for the three new requirements.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex
- test/specled_ex/branch_check/realization_config_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/branch_check_test.exs
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md
- .spec/specs/implementation_tier.spec.md
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/config.ex
- lib/specled_ex/config/realization.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- .spec/specs/config.spec.md
- .spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md
- README.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- No parse-shape changes: if a parse problem surfaces, it goes back through S1's files as an S1 fix, not a drive-by here.
- No changes to mix spec.check task wiring — threading is internal to BranchCheck.
- Do not fix the known implementation-tier AST-shape mismatch (Binding.resolve/2 vs Canonical.normalize/1) — out of scope exactly as it was for specled_-q59.9; use impl-friendly fixtures.
- Orchestrator code untouched (moduledoc only); the default tier list stays owned by the orchestrator.
- Conventions: escape interpolation in doc examples; @impl with explicit behaviour module; to_timeout/1 not :timer helpers.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-213ec781
Log: /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-thread-enabled-tiers-branchcheck/.codex-runs/specled_-s65.2.log

Commit: e0cb642c74b0002971537f683ac1176301e82cfa
Files touched: .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md, .spec/specs/implementation_tier.spec.md, .spec/state.json, lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex, test/specled_ex/branch_check/realization_config_test.exs
Tests added: SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.RealizationConfigTest "enabled_tiers including implementation runs the implementation tier"; "absent realization config keeps the orchestrator default tier set"; "explicit empty enabled_tiers runs no tiers and preserves committed hashes"; "rejected realization tier names emit a branch guard finding that can be disabled"
Verification stubs flipped: none (added execute:true tagged_tests verification coverage for specled.branch_guard.realization_tiers_from_config, specled.branch_guard.realization_unknown_tier_finding, and specled.implementation_tier.config_opt_in)

Summary: BranchCheck now loads .spec/config.yml once, reuses that config for severity resolution, and threads realization.enabled_tiers into the realization orchestrator only when the config explicitly provides a list. Unknown realization tiers now emit branch_guard_realization_unknown_tier findings through the normal severity pipeline, including branch_guard.severities off, and the new tests cover enabled, absent, empty, and rejected-tier config behavior.

VERIFY: FAIL
Session: 80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 6 (lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex, test/specled_ex/branch_check/realization_config_test.exs, .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md, .spec/specs/implementation_tier.spec.md, .spec/state.json)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3 tagged_tests blocks (execute:true) across the two specs; passed: 3 (all backing tests green under `mix test`)
- Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement):
  - specled.branch_guard.realization_tiers_from_config: COVERED by realization_config_test.exs:40 (absent -> asserts api_boundary ran + implementation absent, :50-52), :15 (list -> asserts implementation hash present, :36), :56 (empty -> hashes preserved). Would fail if the nil branch passed [] (api_boundary fetch would be nil) or enabled implementation (refute at :51 fails), or if threading dropped enabled_tiers (no implementation hash at :36).
  - specled.branch_guard.realization_unknown_tier_finding: COVERED by realization_config_test.exs:89. Would fail if default severity != warning (:108), file != .spec/config.yml (:109), message omitted the rejected token / valid-tier list (:110,:112), or if a `branch_guard.severities: off` override did not suppress it (:131 expects []).
  - specled.implementation_tier.config_opt_in: COVERED by realization_config_test.exs:15 (:36 asserts the implementation tier actually produced a hash-store entry via config opt-in, not merely that config parsed). Would fail if config->orchestrator threading broke. The "absent -> tier excluded" clause is exercised by :40/:51 (see Notes re: tag).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. No parse-shape changes to Config/Realization (those files untouched); no mix spec.check task wiring changes (lib/mix/tasks/** untouched); the implementation-tier AST-shape mismatch (Binding.resolve/2 vs Canonical.normalize/1) was NOT touched; orchestrator.ex change is moduledoc-only (single diff hunk, no code).
- Project verification: FAIL (timeout only). `mix spec.check` returns 61 findings, ALL of type verification_command_failed, ALL from the shared whole-suite aggregated `tagged_tests` command exceeding verification.command_timeout_ms (400s). Proven environmental, NOT a ticket defect: `mix test` = 820 tests / 0 failures and `mix test --include integration` = 820 tests / 0 failures when run without the cap. The verifier kills the command at 400s and records exit 1 (verifier.ex:1886-1902); captured output is all passing dots with no F and no ExUnit summary -- the signature of a timeout kill, not a test failure. The 400s cap is set at the base commit (60c2a2f) and this ticket does not touch .spec/config.yml (Out-of-Scope).
- Double-Config.load removal confirmed: YES. Single Config.load(root) at branch_check.ex:43, threaded to severity_opts/3 (branch_check.ex:157 -- no longer loads config itself), run_realization/4 (:198, reads config.realization.enabled_tiers), and realization_unknown_tier_findings/2 (:218, reads config.realization.rejected).
- Empty-enabled_tiers hash-preservation test confirmed: YES. realization_config_test.exs:56 seeds committed api_boundary + implementation hashes, sets enabled_tiers: [], and asserts HashStore.fetch(...) == the seeded raw hash for BOTH tiers (:84-85) -- i.e. it asserts preservation, not merely that no tier ran. Mechanism confirmed at orchestrator.ex:850: refresh_and_commit_hashes/3 skips HashStore.write when the computed realization map is empty (empty enabled set -> empty bindings -> empty map), so committed hashes are never replaced.

Verdict: FAIL

Gaps (if FAIL):
- No requirement-coverage gaps and no scope violations were found. The FAIL verdict is due solely to the mandated merge gate `mix spec.check` returning non-zero. Root cause is an environmental command timeout (aggregated whole-suite tagged_tests > 400s command_timeout_ms), NOT a defect in this ticket -- all 820 tests + integration pass (0 failures) when run without the wall-clock cap. Remediation is outside this ticket's Allowed touches (.spec/config.yml is Out-of-Scope for S2): raise verification.command_timeout_ms, or run spec.check in an environment where the suite completes under the cap. Recommend the team lead accept the ticket's substance and address the timeout separately; the gate cannot be certified green as-is.

Notes (advisory -- do NOT change the verdict):
- implementation_tier.config_opt_in's "absent -> implementation tier excluded" clause is exercised by the absent-config test (realization_config_test.exs:40-52), but that test is tagged only specled.branch_guard.realization_tiers_from_config, not config_opt_in. The failure mode is still caught (both requirements share the same nil-handling code path), and config_opt_in's tagged_tests verification is satisfied by the enabled test at :15. Tagging nuance only.
- The absent-config test proves api_boundary ran and implementation did not -- the operative nil-vs-list distinction. It does not separately assert expanded_behavior/typespecs/use ran, but no ticket code path could produce a partial-default failure mode (the nil branch omits :enabled_tiers entirely, so the orchestrator's full @default_tiers applies wholesale).
- "one branch_guard_realization_unknown_tier finding per rejected entry" is exercised with a single unknown token; the per-entry (plural) behavior is structurally evident from Enum.flat_map over List.wrap(config.realization.rejected) at branch_check.ex:218-228.
- .spec/state.json was regenerated and includes all three new requirement IDs plus the specled.decision.realization_tiers_nil_default reference.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: 80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6.jsonl

Reasoning:

ENVIRONMENTAL-TIMEOUT CLAIM — CONFIRMED independently (not taken on faith):
1. verifier.ex:1882-1902 — on timeout_ms elapse the receive falls through to Port.close(port), exit_status becomes :timeout, and exit_code is hard-set to 1. verifier.ex:392-402 / 1004 / 1058-1063 map any non-zero exit to a verification_command_failed finding. So a timeout kill is byte-for-byte indistinguishable from a real failure in the findings list itself; the only discriminator is re-running the underlying command without the cap (which is what the verifier did).
2. I re-ran `mix test --include integration` in this stage worktree: 820 tests, 2 properties, 0 failures, exit 0 (ExUnit "Finished in 330.7 seconds"; 336s wall). spec.check's merged tagged_tests subprocess (fresh mix boot + 60+ explicit file args, spawned via /bin/sh wrapper) under the concurrent Codex load exceeds the 400s cap where a bare `mix test` at ~330s does not — exactly the variance the timeout theory predicts.
3. git diff <base>..HEAD -- .spec/config.yml is EMPTY. The 400000ms command_timeout_ms was set at base 60c2a2f and this ticket does not touch it (Out of Scope, respected).
4. Corroborating artifact: the working-tree .spec/state.json left by the verifier's own spec.check holds 61 findings, ALL verification_command_failed, ALL the identical merged whole-suite tagged_tests command (seed 898903), each captured output an unbroken run of passing dots that truncates with NO F, NO ExUnit summary, NO stacktrace — the precise signature of a Port.close timeout kill, not test failures. No other finding type present; no real failure is masked.

REQUIREMENT COVERAGE (three new musts, each with a load-bearing assertion that would fail if the requirement were violated):
- specled.branch_guard.realization_tiers_from_config: realization_config_test.exs:15 (list [api_boundary,implementation] -> asserts implementation hash present :36), :40 (nil/absent -> api_boundary present :50 AND implementation key absent :51 AND no implementation-tier finding :52), :56 (empty [] -> both seeded hashes preserved byte-for-byte :84-85). The nil->api_boundary assertion is genuine, not orthogonal: @bare_module is declared only under implementation, and its api_boundary hash appears solely via the implementation=>api_boundary implication (effective_binding.ex:55, orchestrator.ex:473-487) under the default tier set. Threading is load-bearing at branch_check.ex:198-213 (nil omits :enabled_tiers; list forwards it).
- specled.branch_guard.realization_unknown_tier_finding: realization_config_test.exs:89 asserts default warning (:108), file .spec/config.yml (:109), message names rejected token + valid-tier list (:110/:112), AND a branch_guard.severities off override suppresses it (:131 == []). Path verified: emit/Severity.resolve at branch_check.ex:172-189 with default :warning from @per_code_defaults (branch_check.ex:22); :off returns [] at branch_check.ex:176. Per-entry (plural) behavior is a flat_map over List.wrap(config.realization.rejected) at branch_check.ex:218-228 — tested with a single token; acceptable as a Note, the singleton `assert [finding] =` still pins one-per-entry for the base case.
- specled.implementation_tier.config_opt_in: realization_config_test.exs:15 — implementation-hash presence is a genuine proxy for the tier running (seed_uncommitted_hashes -> seed_implementation_subjects at orchestrator.ex:687-693 fires ONLY when :implementation is in the enabled set, which collect_bindings derives from the config-threaded enabled_tiers). NOT a duplicate of realization_tiers_from_config: the latter additionally owns the nil and [] branches (:40, :56). Verifier's tagging Note (absent-clause tagged only realization_tiers_from_config) is a fair nuance — the shared nil-path failure mode is still caught.

EMPTY-TIERS PRESERVATION genuinely asserts PRESERVATION, not merely "no tier ran": HashStore.write REPLACES the whole realization key (hash_store.ex:149-160), so running any single tier on [] would clobber the sibling entry. Test :56 seeds BOTH api_boundary and implementation and asserts both survive byte-for-byte (:84-85). Mechanism: enabled=[] -> collect_bindings returns {} (orchestrator.ex:428-430) -> both seed and refresh_and_commit_hashes compute {} -> HashStore.write never called (orchestrator.ex:702, :836-852). Confirmed.

SCOPE (re-counted against the actual diff, not trusted): 6 files, all inside Allowed globs, 0 Out-of-Scope. git diff <base>..HEAD is empty for config.ex, config/realization.ex, lib/mix/tasks/**, config.spec.md, realized_by.spec.md, README.md, and .spec/config.yml. Single Config.load at branch_check.ex:43 (double-load removed; severity_opts/run_realization now take the struct). orchestrator.ex diff is ONE hunk (@@ -34,15 +34,17 @@) entirely inside the @moduledoc — zero code lines. AST-shape mismatch (Binding.resolve vs Canonical.normalize) untouched; the impl-friendly bare-module fixture (SpecLedEx.Coverage) sidesteps it. Committed .spec/state.json carries all three new requirement IDs + specled.decision.realization_tiers_nil_default. Matches the VERIFY comment's mechanical counts.

Benign note (does NOT affect verdict): my run printed one mid-stream `/bin/sh: specled_cmd_5986.sh: No such file or directory` — a pre-existing temp-script cleanup race in verifier.ex run_command, unrelated to this ticket's files; final tally still 820/0/exit 0.

Verdict: code and tests are sound and in-scope; every new must-requirement is covered by an assertion that fails on violation; no scope violation exists. The sole gate failure (mix spec.check) is the known transient environmental timeout (root cause tracked in specled_-s65.4 and specled_-s65.8), satisfiable by proxy. APPROVE.
APPROVED. Merged into specled_-s65/realization-enabled-tiers. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: S2: Thread enabled_tiers into BranchCheck + unknown-tier findings + tier-run tests
